records.ety: [L. infinitas; pref. in- not + finis boundary, limit, end: cf. F. infinit. See Finite.]

defs: Unlimited extent of time, space, or quantity; eternity; boundlessness; immensity.
quotes: There can not be more infinities than one; for one of them would limit the other.

defs: Unlimited capacity, energy, excellence, or knowledge; as, the infinity of God and his perfections.
Endless or indefinite number; great multitude; as an infinity of beauties.
A quantity greater than any assignable quantity of the same kind.
notes: ☞ Mathematically considered, infinity is always a limit of a variable quantity, resulting from a particular supposition made upon the varying element which enters it.

defs: That part of a line, or of a plane, or of space, which is infinitely distant. In modern geometry, parallel lines or planes are sometimes treated as lines or planes meeting at infinity.
